description: Capture official Tanium method-selection guidance and verified schema/API findings in the plan artifacts before implementation starts.
Introduce a provider-neutral RMM provider adapter contract covering connection status, scope discovery, inventory sync, and optional event/action capabilities.
Introduce an RMM provider registry that drives provider lookup and capability-driven UI rendering instead of hard-coded provider switches.
Add provider capability metadata so shared UI and server logic can distinguish inventory-only providers from providers that also support events, alerts, or remote access.
Extract a shared RMM scope sync path that normalizes external scope records and persists them into rmm_organization_mappings.
Extract a shared RMM asset ingestion path that correlates external devices through tenant_external_entity_mappings and creates or updates Alga assets.
Use tenant_external_entity_mappings as the canonical external identity backbone for all RMM asset syncs.
Keep direct and Temporal execution as pluggable sync transports while reusing the same shared provider business logic.
Wrap NinjaOne behind the new adapter/ingestion seams without changing its current user-visible behavior.
Wrap Tactical RMM behind the new adapter/ingestion seams without changing its current user-visible behavior.
Add Tanium as a supported RMM provider identifier in shared RMM types and persistence code.
Implement Tanium configuration persistence and connection status handling through the shared provider contract.
Implement a Tanium client/query layer that prefers Gateway and uses documented module APIs only for verified capability gaps.
Implement Tanium external scope discovery and mapping to Alga clients through rmm_organization_mappings.
Implement Tanium full device inventory sync for mapped scopes through the shared ingestion pipeline.
Implement a documented Tanium fallback path for aged-out inventory only if Gateway coverage is insufficient and the Asset API is required.
Render Tanium in the RMM settings surface from the provider registry with capability-driven controls and Tanium-specific help text.
Represent Tanium event delivery as an optional provider capability rather than requiring a public inbound webhook route for v1.
Hide unsupported Tanium capabilities such as remote access or remediation actions behind provider capability flags until explicitly implemented.
Emit provider-neutral RMM sync lifecycle events for Tanium using the existing integration/workflow event model.
Document unresolved Tanium schema questions explicitly in the plan and resolve them through tenant-backed schema review before implementation code relies on them.
